columns.name: AADOperationType
columns.description: Type of the operation. Possible values are Add Update Delete and Other.

columns.name: AADTenantId
columns.description: ID of the ADD tenant

columns.name: ActivityDateTime
columns.description: Date and time the activity was performed in UTC.

columns.name: ActivityDisplayName
columns.description: Activity name or the operation name. Examples include Create User and Add member to group. For full list see Azure AD activity list.

columns.name: AdditionalDetails
columns.description: Indicates additional details on the activity.

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: Category
columns.description: Currently Audit is the only supported value.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: Optional GUID that's passed by the client. Can help correlate client-side operations with server-side operations and is useful when tracking logs that span services.

columns.name: DurationMs
columns.description: Property is not used and can be ignored.

columns.name: Id
columns.description: GUID that uniquely identifies the activity.

columns.name: Identity
columns.description: Identity from the token that was presented when the request was made. The identity can be a user account system account or service principal.

columns.name: InitiatedBy
columns.description: User or app initiated the activity.

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: Level
columns.description: Message type. This is currently always Informational.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Location of the datacenter.

columns.name: LoggedByService
columns.description: Service that initiated the activity (For example: Self-service Password Management Core Directory B2C Invited Users Microsoft Identity Manager Privileged Identity Management.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Name of the operation.

columns.name: OperationVersion
columns.description: REST API version that's requested by the client.

columns.name: Result
columns.description: Result of the activity. Possible values are: success failure timeout unknownFutureValue.

columns.name: ResultDescription
columns.description: Additional description of the result.

columns.name: ResultReason
columns.description: Describes cause of failure or timeout results.

columns.name: ResultSignature
columns.description: Property is not used and can be ignored.

columns.name: ResultType
columns.description: Result of the operation. Possible values are Success and Failure.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: TargetResources
columns.description: Indicates information on which resource was changed due to the activity. Target Resource Type can be User Device Directory App Role Group Policy or Other.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time the record was created.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: ActivityStatusValue
columns.description: Status of the operation in display-friendly format. Common values include Started, In Progress, Succeeded, Failed, Active, Resolved.

columns.name: ActivitySubstatusValue
columns.description: Substatus of the operation in display-friendly format. E.g. OK (HTTP Status Code: 200).

columns.name: Authorization
columns.description: Blob of RBAC properties of the event. Usually includes the "action", "role" and "scope" properties. Stored as string. The use of Authorization_d should be preferred going forward.

columns.name: Authorization_d
columns.description: Blob of RBAC properties of the event. Usually includes the "action", "role" and "scope" properties. Stored as dynamic column.

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: Caller
columns.description: GUID of the caller.

columns.name: CallerIpAddress
columns.description: IP address of the user who has performed the operation UPN claim or SPN claim based on availability.

columns.name: CategoryValue
columns.description: Category of the activity log e.g. Administrative, Policy, Security.

columns.name: Claims
columns.description: The JWT token used by Active Directory to authenticate the user or application to perform this operation in Resource Manager. The use of claims_d should be preferred going forward.

columns.name: Claims_d
columns.description: The JWT token used by Active Directory to authenticate the user or application to perform this operation in Resource Manager.

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.description: Usually a GUID in the string format. Events that share a correlationId belong to the same uber action.

columns.name: EventDataId
columns.description: Unique identifier of an event.

columns.name: EventSubmissionTimestamp
columns.description: Timestamp when the event became available for querying.

columns.name: Hierarchy
columns.description: Management group hierarchy of the management group or subscription that event belongs to.

columns.name: HTTPRequest
columns.description: Blob describing the Http Request. Usually includes the "clientRequestId", "clientIpAddress" and "method" (HTTP method. For example, PUT).

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: Level
columns.description: Level of the event. One of the following values: Critical, Error, Warning, Informational and Verbose.

columns.name: OperationId
columns.description: GUID of the operation

columns.name: OperationNameValue
columns.description: Identifier of the operation e.g. Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/listAccountSas/action.

columns.name: Properties
columns.description: Set of <Key Value> pairs (i.e. Dictionary) describing the details of the event. Stored as string. Usage of Properties_d is recommended instead.

columns.name: Properties_d
columns.description: Set of <Key Value> pairs (i.e. Dictionary) describing the details of the event. Stored as dynamic column.

columns.name: ResourceGroup
columns.description: Resource group name of the impacted resource.

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: ResourceProviderValue
columns.description: Id of the resource provider for the impacted resource - e.g. Microsoft.Storage.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: SubscriptionId
columns.description: Subscription ID of the impacted resource.

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

columns.name: TenantId
columns.description: The Log Analytics workspace ID

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp when the event was generated by the Azure service processing the request corresponding the event.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: CollectorHostName
columns.description: Name of the system on which the collector agent is installed.

columns.name: Computer
columns.description: Computer from which the event originated.

columns.name: EventTime
columns.description: Date and time that the event was generated.

columns.name: Facility
columns.description: The part of the system that generated the message.

columns.name: HostIP
columns.description: IP address of the system from which the message originated. Depending on network configuration/topology, this may have a blank or placeholder value, especially when the message originates from a remote device.

columns.name: HostName
columns.description: Name of the system from which the message originated.

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: ProcessID
columns.description: ID of the process that generated the message.

columns.name: ProcessName
columns.description: Name of the process that generated the message.

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: SeverityLevel
columns.description: Severity level of the event.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

columns.name: SyslogMessage
columns.description: Text of the message.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time the record was created.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: AzureDeploymentID
columns.description: Azure deployment ID of the cloud service the log belongs to. Only populated when events are collected using Azure Diagnostics agent when data is pulled from Azure storage.

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: cIP
columns.description: IP address of the client that accessed the web server.

columns.name: Computer
columns.description: Name of the computer that the event was collected from.

columns.name: Confidence
columns.description: Only populated for IIS logs collected from Azure Cloud Services through Azure Diagnostics Extension.

columns.name: csBytes
columns.description: Number of bytes that the server received.

columns.name: csCookie
columns.description: Content of the cookie sent or received if any.

columns.name: csHost
columns.description: Host header name if any.

columns.name: csMethod
columns.description: Method of the request such as GET or POST.

columns.name: csReferer
columns.description: Site that the user last visited. This site provided a link to the current site.

columns.name: csUriQuery
columns.description: The query if any that the client was trying to perform. A Universal Resource Identifier (URI) query is necessary only for dynamic pages.

columns.name: csUriStem
columns.description: Target of the action such as a web page for example Default.htm.

columns.name: csUserAgent
columns.description: Browser type of the client.

columns.name: csUserName
columns.description: Name of the authenticated user that accessed the server. Anonymous users are indicated by a hyphen.

columns.name: csVersion
columns.description: Protocol version that the client used.

columns.name: Description
columns.description: Only populated for IIS logs collected from Azure Cloud Services (through Azure Diagnostics Extension).

columns.name: FirstReportedDateTime
columns.description: Only populated for IIS logs collected from Azure Cloud Services (through Azure Diagnostics Extension).

columns.name: IndicatorThreatType
columns.description: Only populated for IIS logs collected from Azure Cloud Services (through Azure Diagnostics Extension).

columns.name: IsActive
columns.description: Only populated for IIS logs collected from Azure Cloud Services (through Azure Diagnostics Extension).

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: LastReportedDateTime
columns.description: Only populated for IIS logs collected from Azure Cloud Services (through Azure Diagnostics Extension).

columns.name: MaliciousIP
columns.description: Only populated for IIS logs collected from Azure Cloud Services (through Azure Diagnostics Extension).

columns.name: ManagementGroupName
columns.description: Name of the management group for Operations Manager agents. For other agents this is AOI-<workspace ID>.

columns.name: RemoteIPCountry
columns.description: Country/region of the IP address of the client.

columns.name: RemoteIPLatitude
columns.description: Latitude of the client IP address.

columns.name: RemoteIPLongitude
columns.description: Longitude of the client IP address.

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: Role
columns.description: Role instance of the cloud service the log belongs to. Only populated when events are collected using Azure Diagnostics agent and data is pulled from Azure storage.

columns.name: RoleInstance
columns.description: Role of the cloud service the log belongs to. Only populated when events are collected using Azure Diagnostics agent and data is pulled from Azure storage.

columns.name: scBytes
columns.description: Number of bytes that the server sent.

columns.name: scStatus
columns.description: HTTP status code.

columns.name: scSubStatus
columns.description: Substatus error code.

columns.name: scWin32Status
columns.description: Windows status code.

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: Only populated for IIS logs collected from Azure Cloud Services through Azure Diagnostics Extension.

columns.name: sIP
columns.description: IP address of the server on which the log file entry was generated.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: sPort
columns.description: Server port number that is configured for the service.

columns.name: sSiteName
columns.description: Name of the IIS site.

columns.name: StorageAccount
columns.description: Only populated for IIS logs collected from Azure Cloud Services through Azure Diagnostics Extension.

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time the record was created.

columns.name: TimeTaken
columns.description: Length of time to process the request in milliseconds.

columns.name: TLPLevel
columns.description: Only populated for IIS logs collected from Azure Cloud Services through Azure Diagnostics Extension.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: AgentId
columns.description: Unique agent GUID for the agent reporting data on the server.

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: BytesReceived
columns.description: Total number of bytes that have been received during the reporting time window.

columns.name: BytesSent
columns.description: Total number of bytes that have been sent during the reporting time window.

columns.name: Computer
columns.description: Name of the server from the ServiceMapComputer_CL table.

columns.name: Confidence
columns.description: Values are 0 - 100.

columns.name: ConnectionId
columns.description: Unique Id for the connection record.

columns.name: Description
columns.description: Description of the observed threat.

columns.name: DestinationIp
columns.description: IP address of the destination.

columns.name: DestinationPort
columns.description: Port number of the destination.

columns.name: Direction
columns.description: Direction of the connection value is inbound or outbound

columns.name: FirstReportedDateTime
columns.description: The first time the provider reported the indicator.

columns.name: IndicatorThreatType
columns.description: Threat indicator detected. Possible values are Botnet C2 CryptoMining Darknet DDos MaliciousUrl Malware Phishing Proxy PUA Watchlist.

columns.name: IsActive
columns.description: The last time the indicator was seen by Interflow.

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: LastReportedDateTime
columns.description: Indicates indicators are deactivated with True or False value.

columns.name: LinksEstablished
columns.description: Number of physical network connections that have been established during the reporting time window.

columns.name: LinksFailed
columns.description: Number of physical network connections that have failed during the reporting time window. This information is currently available only for outbound connections.

columns.name: LinksLive
columns.description: Number of physical network connections that were open at the end of the reporting time window.

columns.name: LinksTerminated
columns.description: Number of physical network connections that have been terminated during the reporting time window.

columns.name: Machine
columns.description: FQDN of the computer.

columns.name: MaliciousIp
columns.description: Remote IP address.

columns.name: Process
columns.description: Identity of process or groups of processes initiating or accepting the connection.

columns.name: ProcessName
columns.description: Unique identifier for the process in the ServiceMapProcess_CL table.

columns.name: Protocol
columns.description: Protocol used for the connection. Only possible value is tcp.

columns.name: RemoteClassification
columns.description: A classification of the remote endpoint based on its ip and dns names and the corresponding Azure service.

columns.name: RemoteCountry
columns.description: Name of the country or region hosting RemoteIp.

columns.name: RemoteDnsCanonicalNames
columns.description: A JSON array of canonical names that came back from the DNS server. For example when using traffic manager you issue a question to foo.trafficmanage.net and get a canonical name as something.myservice.com together with an ip address.

columns.name: RemoteDnsQuestions
columns.description: A JSON array of DNS questions (lookups) that were performed on the machine and resolved to the RemoteIp listed in the record.

columns.name: RemoteIp
columns.description: The IP address of the remote end of a connection is included in the RemoteIp property. For inbound connections RemoteIp is the same as SourceIp while for outbound connections it is the same as DestinationIp.

columns.name: RemoteLatitude
columns.description: Geolocation latitude. An example would be 47.68.

columns.name: RemoteLongitude
columns.description: Geolocation longitude. An example would be -122.12.

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: Responses
columns.description: Number of responses observed during the reporting time window.

columns.name: ResponseTimeMax
columns.description: Largest response time observed during the reporting time window in milliseconds. If no value the property is blank.

columns.name: ResponseTimeMin
columns.description: Smallest response time observed during the reporting time windowin milliseconds. If no value the property is blank.

columns.name: ResponseTimeSum
columns.description: Sum of all response times observed during the reporting time window in milliseconds. If no value the property is blank.

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: Possible values are 0 - 5 where 5 is the most severe and 0 is not severe at all. Default value is 3.

columns.name: SourceIp
columns.description: IP address of the source.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time the record was created.

columns.name: TLPLevel
columns.description: Traffic Light Protocol (TLP) Level. Possible values are White Green Amber Red.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: ApplicationProtocol
columns.description: Type of network protocol used

columns.name: ApplicationServiceName
columns.description: Hold over field from old schema - attribute not collected

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: Computer
columns.description: Computer name where data was collected

columns.name: Confidence
columns.description: Confidence level for Malicious IP identification. Values are 0 - 100.

columns.name: Description
columns.description: Description of the observed threat.

columns.name: Direction
columns.description: Inbound or outbound

columns.name: FirstReportedDateTime
columns.description: The first time the provider reported the threat.

columns.name: IndicatorThreatType
columns.description: Threat indicator detected is one of the following values Botnet C2 CryptoMining Darknet DDos MaliciousUrl Malware Phishing Proxy PUA Watchlist.

columns.name: IPVersion
columns.description: IP version

columns.name: IsActive
columns.description: Indicates indicators are deactivated with True or False value.

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: LastReportedDateTime
columns.description: The last time the indicator was seen by Interflow.

columns.name: LatencyMilliseconds
columns.description: Hold over field from old schema - attribute not collected

columns.name: LatencySamplingFailureRate
columns.description: Hold over field from old schema - attribute not collected

columns.name: LatencySamplingTimeStamp
columns.description: Hold over field from old schema - attribute not collected

columns.name: LocalIP
columns.description: IP address of the local computer

columns.name: LocalMAC
columns.description: Hold over field from old schema - attribute not collected

columns.name: LocalPortNumber
columns.description: Local port number

columns.name: LocalSubnet
columns.description: Subnet where data was collected

columns.name: MaliciousIP
columns.description: IP address of a known malicious source

columns.name: ManagementGroupName
columns.description: Name of the Operations Manager management group

columns.name: ProcessID
columns.description: Windows process ID

columns.name: ProcessName
columns.description: Path and file name of the process

columns.name: ProtocolName
columns.description: Name of the network protocol used

columns.name: ReceivedBytes
columns.description: Amount of bytes received

columns.name: ReceivedPackets
columns.description: Hold over field from old schema - attribute not collected

columns.name: RemoteIP
columns.description: Remote IP address used by the remote computer

columns.name: RemoteIPCountry
columns.description: Country/region of the remote IP address

columns.name: RemoteIPLatitude
columns.description: IP latitude value

columns.name: RemoteIPLongitude
columns.description: IP longitude value

columns.name: RemoteMAC
columns.description: Hold over field from old schema - attribute not collected

columns.name: RemotePortNumber
columns.description: Port number used by the remote IP address

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: SentBytes
columns.description: Number of bytes sent

columns.name: SentPackets
columns.description: Hold over field from old schema - attribute not collected

columns.name: SequenceNumber
columns.description: Hold over field from old schema - attribute not collected

columns.name: SessionEndTime
columns.description: End time of session

columns.name: SessionID
columns.description: A unique value that identifies communication session between two IP addresses

columns.name: SessionStartTime
columns.description: Start time of session

columns.name: SessionState
columns.description: Connected or disconnected

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: Suspected malware severity

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Time of the record

columns.name: TLPLevel
columns.description: Traffic Light Protocol (TLP) Level is one of the defined values White Green Amber Red.

columns.name: TotalBytes
columns.description: Total number of bytes sent during session

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table
